description: The fine solid residue left after material burns is 'hesura'. Its composition depends on what burned and how completely. 'muhena' (dust) covers fine settled particles regardless of origin.
articulatory_notes: An open glottis releases unvoiced 'h' into front 'e'. A narrow groove along the tongue directs 's' into stressed rounded 'u'; a tap or brief trill then opens into final 'a'.
usage_notes: 'hesura' needs no classifier when ash is treated as a mass. 'thero hesura' is the ash from a fire; a source noun can narrow it to wood ash or another residue.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Wood ash can enter soapmaking, glaze, or soil work. Ash from painted or contaminated material carries a different risk, so the practical knowledge begins with what burned.

semantic_domains.nature: residue remaining after natural or tended fire
semantic_domains.physical: fine solid material left by combustion
